metadata.source: MOT + ZATCA + TGA + SAL + MODON + Various Free Zone Authorities
metadata.source_ar: وزارة النقل والخدمات اللوجستية + هيئة الزكاة والضريبة والجمارك + الهيئة العامة للنقل + الشركة السعودية للخدمات اللوجستية + مدن + هيئات المناطق الحرة
metadata.notes: Saudi Arabia logistics and transport sector regulations, costs, and requirements as of Q1 2025. Part of Vision 2030 National Transport and Logistics Strategy (NTLS).

mot_transport_licenses.notes: All transport licenses require a valid CR (Commercial Registration) from MCI as a prerequisite. Managed via the TGA Unified Portal.

requirements: Valid Commercial Registration (CR) with freight transport activity code
Minimum fleet of 5 trucks meeting SASO safety standards
GPS/AVL tracking system installed on all vehicles
Third-party liability insurance (minimum 500,000 SAR coverage)
Driver qualification cards (Istimara for heavy vehicles)
MHRSD labor office registration
35% Saudization ratio compliance
items.notes_en: Covers intercity and cross-regional heavy freight transport. Vehicles must pass Fahas (periodic inspection) every 6 months.

requirements: Valid CR with delivery services activity code
Minimum 10 delivery vehicles or motorcycles
Real-time tracking system for all delivery units
Delivery driver training certification
Third-party liability insurance
Customer complaint handling system
CITC compliance for delivery app (if app-based)
items.notes_en: Covers last-mile and same-day intra-city delivery. Includes motorcycle couriers and van-based delivery fleets.

requirements: Valid CR with warehousing activity code
Municipal license (Balady) for warehouse premises
Fire safety certificate from Civil Defense
WMS (Warehouse Management System) implementation
Compliance with SASO storage standards
Environmental impact assessment for large facilities (>5,000 sqm)
SFDA license if storing food or pharmaceuticals
items.notes_en: Warehousing license covers 3PL storage, fulfillment centers, and bonded warehouses. Additional SFDA approval needed for food/pharma storage.

items.authority: ZATCA - Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority
requirements: Valid CR with customs brokerage activity code
Licensed customs broker with ZATCA certification exam
Bank guarantee of 100,000 SAR
Office premises near customs point or port
Electronic connectivity to ZATCA Fasah system
Professional indemnity insurance
Clean record (no customs violations in 3 years)
items.notes_en: Customs brokers must pass the ZATCA certification exam. All declarations processed via the Fasah single-window platform.

requirements: Valid CR with freight forwarding activity code
FIATA membership or equivalent international accreditation
Bank guarantee of 50,000 SAR
Cargo liability insurance (minimum 1,000,000 SAR)
Qualified staff with logistics certifications
Electronic integration with carrier systems
35% Saudization ratio compliance
items.notes_en: Covers arranging shipments by air, sea, and land on behalf of shippers. FIATA accreditation preferred but not mandatory.

items.authority_ar: الهيئة العامة للنقل + هيئة الغذاء والدواء
requirements: Valid CR with cold chain transport activity code
Refrigerated vehicles with continuous temperature monitoring
SFDA cold chain compliance certification
Temperature data logging and 90-day record retention
Staff trained in cold chain handling (SFDA-approved courses)
Emergency contingency plan for equipment failure
Calibrated temperature sensors (annual calibration certificate)
items.notes_en: Mandatory for perishable food, dairy, pharmaceutical, and vaccine transport. SFDA conducts unannounced inspections.

requirements: Valid CR with hazardous materials transport code
ADR-certified drivers (Dangerous Goods driving certificate)
Specialized hazmat vehicles with UN-standard markings
Emergency response plan approved by Civil Defense
Hazmat liability insurance (minimum 2,000,000 SAR)
Route approval from TGA for each shipment corridor
MSDS (Material Safety Data Sheets) for all transported materials
Spill containment equipment on each vehicle
items.notes_en: Covers chemicals, petroleum products, gases, and explosives transport. Routes must avoid residential areas. 24/7 emergency hotline required.

requirements: Valid CR with moving services activity code
Minimum 3 enclosed trucks
Cargo insurance covering customer goods
Trained staff for furniture handling and packing
Standard service contract template approved by MCI
Customer complaint resolution system
items.notes_en: Covers household and commercial moving services. Standard contracts must include itemized inventory, valuation, and damage liability clauses.

items.authority_ar: الهيئة العامة للنقل + هيئة الاتصالات وتقنية المعلومات
requirements: Valid CR with courier/postal services code
CITC postal services license
Minimum 15 delivery vehicles
Nationwide coverage plan (or regional coverage declaration)
Real-time parcel tracking system
Customer service center with defined SLAs
Data privacy compliance per PDPL
items.notes_en: Combined TGA transport and CITC postal license required. Must comply with Saudi Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) for customer data.

requirements: Valid CR with port operations activity code
Minimum paid-up capital of 50,000,000 SAR
International port operation experience (5+ years)
ISPS Code compliance (International Ship and Port Facility Security)
Environmental impact assessment approval
Concession agreement with Mawani
Heavy port equipment (cranes, reach stackers, terminal tractors)
Integrated Terminal Operating System (TOS)
items.notes_en: Major concession-based license. Awarded via competitive tender. Typical concession periods are 25-30 years.

logistics_hub_zones.notes: Key ports and dry ports under the National Transport and Logistics Strategy (NTLS). Warehouse costs are average market rates.

items.hub_name_ar: ميناء الملك عبدالله - مدينة الملك عبدالله الاقتصادية
items.notes_en: Privately operated mega-port. Part of KAEC Free Zone with customs duty advantages.
Largest port on the Red Sea. Handles majority of Hajj/Umrah logistics and consumer imports for the Western Region.
Primary gateway for the Eastern Province and central Saudi Arabia via rail. Connected to Dry Port Riyadh by SAR railway.

items.hub_name_en: Dry Port Riyadh (SAR Inland Container Depot)
items.notes_en: Inland container depot connected to Dammam Port via SAR railway. Customs clearance available on-site. Key hub for Riyadh distribution.
Serves SABIC, Saudi Aramco, and other petrochemical giants in Jubail Industrial City. Specialized in liquid bulk and project cargo.
Serves the Jazan Economic City and southwestern region. Growing role in Red Sea trade corridor and African trade routes.

zatca_customs_categories.notes: Based on GCC Common External Tariff (CET). VAT at 15% applies on CIF value plus customs duty. Processed via ZATCA Fasah platform.

items.category_en: Import Duty Rates by Product Category
items.notes_en: Most goods carry 5% CET duty. Selective excise tax applies in addition to customs duty on tobacco, energy drinks, and sugary beverages.
15% VAT applied at point of import. Recoverable as input VAT for registered businesses. Deferral scheme available for authorized economic operators (AEO).

items.category_ar: التعرفة الجمركية الموحدة لدول مجلس التعاون
requirements: GCC Certificate of Origin
Minimum 40% GCC value-added content
items.notes_en: Goods originating from GCC member states (UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman, Qatar) enter duty-free with valid GCC Certificate of Origin.
Temporary imports require a customs bond or bank guarantee. Goods must be re-exported within the allowed period or full duties apply.

requirements: Goods must remain in original condition (or processed under inward processing relief)
Re-export declaration via Fasah within 12 months of import
Duty drawback claim submitted within 18 months
Original import declaration reference number required
items.notes_en: Full duty drawback available for goods re-exported in original condition. Partial drawback for goods processed under inward processing.

free_zone_benefits.notes: Special economic zones offering tax and customs incentives under the Saudi Special Economic Zones framework.

items.zone_name_ar: المنطقة الحرة بمدينة الملك عبدالله الاقتصادية
benefits: 0% corporate income tax for up to 50 years
0% personal income tax
0% customs duty on imports within the zone
0% withholding tax on repatriation of profits
Streamlined business licensing (single-window)
items.customs_advantages: Duty-free import of raw materials and equipment. Duties apply only on goods entering the Saudi customs territory.
items.notes_en: KAEC is Saudi Arabia's first designated Special Economic Zone. Ideal for manufacturing, logistics, and light industry.

items.zone_name_en: Jazan City for Primary and Downstream Industries (JCPDI)
benefits: 0% corporate income tax for up to 50 years
Subsidized industrial land and utilities
Customs duty exemption on imported raw materials
Proximity to Jazan Port for direct import/export
Government-funded infrastructure
items.customs_advantages: Bonded zone status. Duty suspension on materials used for manufacturing and re-export.
items.notes_en: Focus on mining, minerals processing, and energy-intensive industries. Benefits from proximity to raw material sources.

benefits: 0% corporate income tax for up to 50 years
100% foreign ownership permitted
Customs duty exemption
Proximity to Prince Mohammad bin Abdulaziz Airport
Focus on knowledge-based and tech industries
items.customs_advantages: Duty-free import for zone-based operations. Standard tariffs on goods leaving the zone into Saudi customs territory.
items.notes_en: Focus on tech, education, and knowledge-based industries. Unique positioning serving Hajj/Umrah tourism logistics.

benefits: Customs duty deferral until goods enter domestic market
VAT deferral on goods stored within zone
Consolidation and value-added services (labeling, kitting, packaging)
Simplified re-export procedures
24/7 customs clearance services
items.customs_advantages: Duty and VAT deferred until goods are released into Saudi domestic market. No duty on re-exported goods.
items.notes_en: Located near King Khalid International Airport. Designed for e-commerce fulfillment, 3PL, and distribution. Not a full SEZ tax holiday zone.

ecommerce_logistics_requirements.notes: Regulated under the E-Commerce Law (Royal Decree M/126) and MCI E-Commerce regulations. Enforced by MCI and TGA.

rules: Delivery time commitment must be clearly stated at checkout
Delayed delivery beyond stated window entitles customer to compensation or cancellation
Real-time tracking link must be provided via SMS or email
Delivery attempt must be documented with timestamp and GPS coordinates
Failed delivery must trigger customer notification within 1 hour
7-day unconditional return right for e-commerce purchases (E-Commerce Law Article 12)
Seller must provide free return shipping for defective goods
Return pickup must be offered (not only drop-off)
Refund must be processed within 15 business days of return receipt
Return policy must be displayed prominently on the store
COD amounts must be deposited to merchant within 5 business days
Driver must carry POS terminal for electronic COD option
Maximum COD value per shipment: 5,000 SAR
COD reconciliation report provided to merchant daily
Cash handling insurance required for logistics provider
Standard parcel max weight: 30 kg (last-mile delivery)
Oversize freight: above 30 kg requires freight transport license
Maximum single dimension: 150 cm for standard courier
Lithium battery shipments must comply with ICAO/IATA DG regulations
Packaging must comply with SASO marking and labeling standards
Logistics provider must carry cargo liability insurance
Minimum coverage: declared value of goods or 100 SAR/kg (whichever is higher)
Insurance certificate must be available for customer inspection on request
High-value shipments (>10,000 SAR) require additional declared-value coverage
Third-party liability insurance mandatory for all delivery vehicles

salary_benchmarks.sector_name_ar: معايير الرواتب في قطاع الخدمات اللوجستية
salary_benchmarks.notes: Average monthly salaries in SAR for logistics roles in major Saudi cities (Riyadh, Jeddah, Dammam). Based on 2024-2025 market data. Saudization (Nitaqat) requirement for logistics sector is 35%.

items.notes_en: Higher pay for Saudi drivers due to Nitaqat incentives. Many companies offer performance bonuses per delivery.
Expat drivers typically receive housing and transport allowances in addition to base salary.
ZATCA certification holders command higher salaries. Bilingual (Arabic/English) is required.
High demand role. CSCP or APICS certification adds 15-20% salary premium.
